description: Build a portable weather station that records temperature, humidity and barometric pressure to an SD card every minute, runs on battery, and uploads daily summaries over WiFi.
rationale: ESP32 (not Arduino) for WiFi. BME280 covers all three measurements. MicroSD for logging. Battery for portability. Buck converter likely needed for 7.4V battery.

description: Simple plant monitor that beeps when the soil is dry. Just detect dryness and alert me. No connectivity needed.
rationale: Catalog has no soil moisture sensor. Plan should mention this gap rather than substitute. Arduino is sufficient for the buzzer logic.

description: A desk clock that shows time on a small OLED screen, with color-changing LED lighting around it.
rationale: Catalog has no real-time clock module — should be flagged. OLED for display, NeoPixel ring for lighting.

description: Garage parking sensor that uses ultrasonic distance and shows how close the car is on a screen.
A simple motion-triggered alarm. When someone walks in front of it, beep loudly.
Automated curtain opener using a stepper motor that runs on a schedule.
Pet feeder that dispenses food twice a day by rotating a servo-driven hopper. Runs on USB power.
Wearable that detects sudden falls using an accelerometer and signals via a buzzer.
Train and run a small image classifier in Python that identifies objects in front of a camera in real time.
rationale: Image classification needs Linux + Python libs; only RPi qualifies. Catalog has no camera — should be flagged.

description: Outdoor data logger that records temperature once an hour and runs entirely from a small solar panel.
WiFi-controlled smart switch to turn a lamp on or off from my phone.
Pan-tilt camera mount controlled by two small servos.
rationale: Should recommend SG90 micro servo with quantity 2.

description: A small robot car that drives forward and turns away from obstacles using ultrasonic distance sensing.
rationale: Catalog has no DC motor or motor driver — gap should be flagged.

description: Send my room temperature to a cloud dashboard every five minutes via WiFi.
Programmable message board that displays text on a 16x2 character LCD.
Hallway light that turns on automatically when someone walks past at night.
Desk decoration with animated rainbow LED lighting.
Logger for a remote location with no WiFi available. Records temperature and humidity to SD card for two weeks on battery.
rationale: No-WiFi requirement → Arduino is fine; ESP32 not needed.

description: Simple wearable step counter using accelerometer data shown on a small screen.
Records when my garage door opens and closes using a motion sensor, saves timestamps to SD card.
Tiny two-joint robot arm controlled by servos, with manual buttons.
Linux home server that runs a small web dashboard showing my house's IoT sensor data.
Beginner project: a button that lights up a single LED and beeps. Nothing more.
Outdoor decorative ring of color-changing LEDs powered by a solar panel.
Show a real-time distance reading from an ultrasonic sensor on a small character display.
